name: Peach Pie Filling--Bernardin
recipeIngredient: 12 Cups Prepared Peaches, Approximately 20-25 Medium, Weighing About 5 to 6 Lbs
2 Cups Apples, Peeled and Finely Chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch, peel, pit, and slice peaches; measure 12 cups (3 L).
2. Tie cinnamon stick and whole cloves in a square piece of cheesecloth to create a spice bag.
3. Combine the peaches, spice bag, sugar, apples, raisins, lemon juice, vinegar, lemon rind, and nutmeg in a large stainless steel or enamel saucepan.
4. Bring mixture to a boil; cover and continue boiling gently for 60-75 minutes, stirring occasionally until thickened.
5. Fill a boiling water canner with water.
6. Place 4 clean pint (500 ml) mason jars in the canner over high heat.
7. Submerge snap lids in boiling water for 5 minutes to soften the sealing compound.
8. Remove spice bag.
9. Pour pie filling into hot jars, leaving at least 1/2 inch (1 cm) of head space.
10. Remove air bubbles by tapping jars and wiping the rim; remove any stickiness.
11. Center snap lid on each jar and tighten screw band until fingertip tight.
12. Place jars back in the canner.
13. Repeat steps for remaining pie fillings.
14. Cover the canner; return water to a boil.
15. Process 15 minutes at altitudes up to 1,000 ft.
16. Remove jars and allow them to cool.
17. Check jar seals by pressing down on the lids; sealed lids will curve inward.
18. Wipe jars clean, label, and store in a cool, dark place.
Yield: 4 Pint (500 mL) Jars
PEACH PIE:
Before using a jar of peach pie filling to make a pie, add 2 tablespoons cornstarch to each pint jar; stir until well combined. Each jar provides enough filling for an 8-inch (22 cm) pie.
